segments.text: JIM: Hello, South City Cycling Club.
segments.translation: 吉姆：你好，南城骑行俱乐部。

segments.text: WOMAN: Oh, hi. Er ... I want to find out about joining the club.
segments.translation: 女士：噢，嗨。嗯……我想了解一下关于加入俱乐部的事。

segments.text: JIM: Right. I can help you there.
segments.translation: 吉姆：是的。我可以帮你。

segments.text: I'm the club secretary and my name's Jim Hunter.
segments.translation: 我是俱乐部秘书，我叫吉姆·亨特。

segments.text: WOMAN: Oh, hi Jim.
segments.translation: 女士：哦，嗨，吉姆。

segments.text: JIM: So, are you interested in membership for yourself?
segments.translation: 吉姆：那么，你有兴趣成为会员吗？

segments.text: WOMAN: That's right.
segments.translation: 女士：没错。

segments.text: JIM: OK. Well there are basically two types of adult membership.
segments.translation: 吉姆：好的。基本上有两种成人会员。

segments.text: If you're pretty serious about cycling, there's the Full membership.
segments.translation: 如果你是认真对待骑行这件事情的，我们有正式会员资格。

segments.text: That costs 260 dollars and that covers you not just for ordinary cycling but also for races both here in the city and also in other parts of Australia.
segments.translation: 那要花260美元，不仅包括普通的骑行，还包括这里的、城里的以及澳大利亚其他地方的比赛。

segments.text: WOMAN: Right. Well, I'm not really up to that standard.
segments.translation: 女士：是的。嗯，我还没有达到那个标准。

segments.text: I was more interested in just joining a group to do some cycling in my free time.
segments.translation: 我更感兴趣的是在我的空闲时间加入一个小组骑骑车。

segments.text: JIM: Sure. That's why most people join.
segments.translation: 吉姆：当然。这就是大多数人加入的原因。

segments.text: So, in that case you'd be better with the Recreational membership.
segments.translation: 所以，在这种情况下，你最好是办休闲会员。

segments.text: That's 108 dollars if you're over 19, and 95 dollars if you're under.
segments.translation: 十九岁以上是108美元，十九岁以下是95美元。

segments.text: WOMAN: I'm 25.
segments.translation: 女士：我25岁。

segments.text: JIM: OK.
segments.translation: 吉姆：好的。

segments.text: It's paid quarterly, and you can upgrade it later to the Full membership if you want to, of course.
segments.translation: 它是按季度付费的，当然，如果您愿意，您可以在以后将其升级为正式会员。

segments.text: Now both types of membership include the club fee of 20 dollars.
segments.translation: 现在两种类型的会员都包括20美元的俱乐部费。

segments.text: They also provide insurance in case you have an accident, though we hope you won't need that, of course.
segments.translation: 我们也提供保险以防意外，当然我们希望你不需要保险。

segments.text: WOMAN: No. OK, well, I'll go with the Recreational membership, I think.
segments.translation: 女士：没错。好吧，我想我还是选休闲会员吧。

segments.text: And that allows me to join in the club activities, and so on?
segments.translation: 然后这样我就可以参加俱乐部的活动等等，对吧？

segments.text: JIM: That's right.
segments.translation: 吉姆：没错。

segments.text: And once you're a member of the club, you're also permitted to wear our kit when you're out cycling.
segments.translation: 一旦你成为俱乐部的一员，你也可以在骑自行车的时候穿我们的装备。

segments.text: It's green and white.
segments.translation: 它是绿色和白色的。

segments.text: WOMAN: Yes, I've seen cyclists wearing it.
segments.translation: 女士：是的，我看过骑行者们戴着它。

segments.text: So, can I buy that at the club?
segments.translation: 所以我可以在俱乐部买到吗？

segments.text: JIM: No, it's made to order by a company in Brisbane.
segments.translation: 吉姆：不，是布里斯班的一家公司定做的。

segments.text: You can find them online: they're called Jerriz.
segments.translation: 你可以在网上找到他们：他们公司叫杰里兹。

segments.text: That's J-E-R-R-l-Z.
segments.translation: 拼写作J-E-R-R-l-Z。

segments.text: You can use your membership number to put in an order on their website.
segments.translation: 你可以用你的会员号在他们的网站上下单。

segments.text: WOMAN: OK. Now, can you tell me a bit about the rides I can do?
segments.translation: 女士：好的。现在你能告诉我一些关于我可以参加的一些骑行路线吗？

segments.text: JIM: Sure. So we have training rides pretty well every morning, and they're a really good way of improving your cycling skills as well as your general level of fitness, but they're different levels.
segments.translation: 吉姆：当然。我们每天早上都有训练式的骑行活动，这是一种很好的方式来提高你的骑行技能以及你的整体健身水平，但它们有不同的水平。

segments.text: Level A is pretty fast - you're looking at about 30 or 35 kilometres an hour.
segments.translation: 档位A相当快——你的速度大约是每小时30或35公里。

segments.text: If you can do about 25 kilometres an hour, you'd probably be level B, and then level C are the novices, who stay at about 15 kilometres per hour.
segments.translation: 如果你能达到每小时25公里的速度，你很可能在B级水平， 然后C级是新手，他们的速度保持在每小时15公里左右。

segments.text: WOMAN: Right. Well I reckon I'd be level B.
segments.translation: 女士：是的。我想我能达到B级。

segments.text: So, when are the sessions for that level?
segments.translation: 那么，这一级骑行的时段都是什么时候？

segments.text: JIM: There are a couple each week.
segments.translation: 吉姆：每周有几个时段。

segments.text: They're both early morning sessions.
segments.translation: 都是早上很早的时候。

segments.text: There's one on Tuesdays, and for that one you meet at 5.30 am, and the meeting point's the stadium - do you know where that is?
segments.translation: 星期二有一个时段，你们早上五点半见，集合地点在体育场，你知道在哪里吗？

segments.text: WOMAN: Yes, it's quite near my home, in fact.
segments.translation: 女士：是的，事实上那儿离我家很近。

segments.text: OK, and how about the other one?
segments.translation: 好，那另一个呢？

segments.text: JIM: That's on Thursdays.
segments.translation: 吉姆：另外一个是星期四。

segments.text: It starts at the same time, but they meet at the main gate to the park.
segments.translation: 它在同一时间开始，但是他们在公园的大门相遇。

segments.text: WOMAN: Is that the one just past the shopping mall?
segments.translation: 女士：是那条刚好经过购物中心的线路吗？

segments.text: JIM: That's it.
segments.translation: 吉姆：就是这样。

segments.text: WOMAN: So how long are the rides?
segments.translation: 女士：所以这些行程要多久呢？

segments.text: JIM: They're about an hour and a half.
segments.translation: 吉姆：大约一个半小时。

segments.text: So, if you have a job it's easy to fit in before you go to work.
segments.translation: 所以，如果你有工作，那么在你工作前你可以进行这个骑行，很容易安排时间。

segments.text: And the members often go somewhere for coffee afterwards, so it's quite a social event.
segments.translation: 而且会员们经常会在骑行后去某些地方喝咖啡，所以这是一项社交活动。

segments.text: WOMAN: OK. That sounds good.
segments.translation: 女士：好的。听起来不错。

segments.text: I've only just moved to the city so I don't actually know many people yet.
segments.translation: 我刚搬到这个城市，所以还不认识很多人。

segments.text: JIM: Well, it's a great way to meet people.
segments.translation: 吉姆：嗯，这是认识人的好方法。

segments.text: WOMAN: And does each ride have a leader?
segments.translation: 女士：每趟骑行都有领队吗？

segments.text: JIM: Sometimes, but not always.
segments.translation: 吉姆：有时有，但不总是。

segments.text: But you don't really need one; the group members on the ride support one another, anyway.
segments.translation: 但你真的不需要领队；不管怎么说，行程中的小组成员会相互支持的。

segments.text: WOMAN: How would we know where to go?
segments.translation: 女士：我们怎么知道要往哪里骑？

segments.text: JIM: If you check the club website, you'll see that the route for each ride is clearly marked.
segments.translation: 吉姆：如果你查一下俱乐部的网站，你会发现每次骑行的路线都被清楚地标记着。

segments.text: So you can just print that out and take it along with you.
segments.translation: 你可以把它打印出来随身携带。

segments.text: It's similar from one week to another, but it's not always exactly the same.
segments.translation: 每个星期都差不多，但并不总是完全一样。

segments.text: WOMAN: And what do I need to bring?
segments.translation: 女士：我还需要带什么？

segments.text: JIM: Well, bring a bottle of water, and your phone.
segments.translation: 吉姆：嗯，带上一瓶水和你的手机。

segments.text: You shouldn't use it while you're cycling, but have it with you.
segments.translation: 你不能在骑车的时候用它，但要随身携带。

segments.text: WOMAN: Right.
segments.translation: 女士：好的。

segments.text: JIM: And in winter, it's well before sunrise when we set out, so you need to make sure your bike's got lights.
segments.translation: 吉姆：而且在冬天，我们出发的时候太阳还没升起，所以你得确保你的自行车有灯。

segments.text: WOMAN: That's OK. Well, thanks Jim.
segments.translation: 女士：没关系。好，谢谢吉姆。

segments.text: I'd definitely like to join.
segments.translation: 我当然愿意加入。

segments.text: So what's the best way of going about it?
segments.translation: 那么最好的方法是什么呢？

segments.text: JIM: You can ...
segments.translation: 吉姆：你可以……
